decisions are affected the students,due to a paper leak they were planned to conduct and re-examination for the student of 11th this was too unfair that affect the student who are not having any knowledge about the paper leak and forced to write an examination again with the same portion where we read all before the common examination with this with this portion we have already written an examination as quarterly, half yearly and revision 1 and 2 and as well as government annual common examination this is too stressful for the students, all are forced to the examination, they were plan to start 12th special classes we have an holidays between after common examination but because of this re-examination we didn't get any sort of holidays for our relaxation this too too unfair, i hope that management will sort out my concern

itemReviewed.name: G R T Mahalakshmi Vidyalaya - Ashok Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
The situation at GRT Mahalakshmi Vidyalaya is a clear example of poor management and complete disregard for students.

Due to a paper leak — something students had absolutely no role in — the school has forced us to rewrite multiple 11th final exams, including subjects that were never even affected. Instead of handling the issue responsibly, the school has chosen the easiest option: making students suffer for its own failure.

What makes this worse is the lack of transparency. Students were not even properly informed about the paper leak, yet we are the ones facing the consequences. After finishing our exams on the 24th, we were supposed to have a short break before 12th-grade portions begin on April 6. That break has now been taken away, with re-exams scheduled on the 28th, 30th, 1st, and 2nd.

For months, students have already been dealing with nonstop exams — half-yearly, followed by multiple revision tests — often without adequate breaks. Now, being forced to repeat the same exams again due to the school’s inability to manage its own system is not just frustrating, it is completely unfair.

This is not about maintaining academic standards. This is about shifting the consequences of institutional failure onto students. It reflects a serious lack of accountability, poor decision-making, and zero concern for student well-being.

Students deserve better than this kind of treatment. This is not discipline or quality education — it is mismanagement at its worst.

itemReviewed.name: G R T Mahalakshmi Vidyalaya - Ashok Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody:
I am extremely disappointed with how the school has handled the recent examination situation. Due to a paper leak, students have been asked to rewrite multiple 11th final exams — even for subjects that were not affected. This decision feels unreasonable and unfair to students who had no involvement in the issue.

What makes this more frustrating is that students were not even informed about the paper leak beforehand, and yet we are being made to face the consequences. Our final exams had just ended on the 24th, and instead of getting the expected break before starting 12th-grade portions on April 6, we are now forced to write exams again on the 28th, 30th, 1st, and 2nd.

Over the past few months, we have already written multiple exams, including half-yearly and several revision tests, often without proper breaks. This constant pressure is exhausting, and taking away the small break we were looking forward to has only added to the stress.

Students deserve rest and fair treatment. Making us rewrite exams repeatedly for something that was not our fault shows a lack of consideration for our mental and physical well-being. I sincerely hope the school rethinks such decisions in the future and prioritizes students' welfare.

itemReviewed.name: G R T Mahalakshmi Vidyalaya - Ashok Nagar

itemListElement.reviewBody: I would personally rate this school 1.5 stars but since there isn't an option I'll go with 2. And the reason behind this is mainly because of some teachers.I have studied in this school for years and I've seen a lot of teachers, and personally teachers in primary and kg are much better than those who are in high school. Mainly the tamil teacher kalaivani, she has no patience and only wants to scold the students. She acts like a sadist. And whenever someone complaints about her to the principal. She'll just target them for the whole year. And she'll bring her qualification and her experience in teaching, and also questions how we could complaint about her when she does something wrong.i really feel bad for her students.
